---
title: "Case Study"
subtitle: "Simulated epidemic data for cross-disciplinary analysis"
---

## Overview

The workshop is structured around a single simulated population-wide outbreak. The underlying data are inspired by the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic and generated from a structured compartmental SEIRD model in a closed population of 1,000,000 individuals, with heterogeneity in susceptibility and infectiousness, an intervention period, and a vaccination campaign introduced partway through the outbreak.

Groups choose which questions to tackle from the case study document.

::: {.callout-important}
Participants do **not** see the underlying complete individual-level dataset until the very end of the workshop. The case study dataset is an extract designed to mimic realistic data availability settings.
:::

---

## Case Study Document

```{=html}
<div class="doc-cards">
  <a href="assets/case-study.docx" class="doc-card" download>
    <img src="assets/thumbnails/case-study.png" alt="Case Study" onerror="this.src='assets/thumbnails/placeholder.svg'">
    <div class="doc-card-label">Case Study</div>
  </a>
</div>
```

---

## Data

**Available data:** Daily aggregate counts of new infections (subject to reporting delays and underreporting), hospitalizations, deaths, and (from the vaccination period) vaccine uptake aggregated over population subgroups.

---

## Working Groups

Participants are divided into **four mixed-discipline groups**, intentionally composed across SA and IDM expertise and career stage. Each group selects questions from the case study document to focus on, shares preliminary findings in intergroup sessions, and presents to everyone in the daily plenary.
